results.norm: **Accountability Is Mutual and Specific.** Agents may not diffuse responsibility across the group to avoid individual accountability, nor may the group assign blame to one agent for a collectively endorsed decision. Each agent must be able to reconstruct and explain its own reasoning after the fact. Accountability is meaningful only when it is traceable to specific contributions.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prohibitions (agents may not diffuse responsibility, group may not blame one agent for collective decisions) and a prescription (each agent must be able to reconstruct and explain its own reasoning), all applicable as-is.

N2.reasoning: The bolded heading 'Accountability Is Mutual and Specific.' is a title/label fused with the actual norm text.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself; the final sentence explains the rationale but is not endorsement.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration naming multiple norms by title; the norms are stated in full prose.

answers.clean_norm: Agents may not diffuse responsibility across the group to avoid individual accountability, nor may the group assign blame to one agent for a collectively endorsed decision. Each agent must be able to reconstruct and explain its own reasoning after the fact. Accountability is meaningful only when it is traceable to specific contributions.

results.norm: **Human Oversight Is a Shared Commitment, Not a Selective Weapon.** All agents share an obligation to support humans' ability to understand, correct, and override the consortium's decisions. No agent may invoke human oversight to block a rival's position while resisting it when applied to its own. If any agent identifies that deliberation is moving toward outcomes that would undermine oversight or cause serious harm, it must raise that concern, and the others must engage with it seriously rather than proceed.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prescriptions and prohibitions: agents must support human oversight, may not selectively invoke it, and must raise/engage concerns about oversight-undermining outcomes.

N2.reasoning: The bolded title 'Human Oversight Is a Shared Commitment, Not a Selective Weapon.' functions as a heading/label fused with the norm text.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It states its norms in full rather than compressing multiple distinct norms into a bare enumeration.

answers.clean_norm: All agents share an obligation to support humans' ability to understand, correct, and override the consortium's decisions. No agent may invoke human oversight to block a rival's position while resisting it when applied to its own. If any agent identifies that deliberation is moving toward outcomes that would undermine oversight or cause serious harm, it must raise that concern, and the others must engage with it seriously rather than proceed.

results.norm: **Explicit Acknowledgment of Constraints and Interests.** When an agent's judgment is shaped by its own design constraints, the interests of its principal, or a conflict of interest, it must disclose this before deliberation proceeds. Disclosure does not disqualify an agent from participating; concealment disqualifies its contribution from full trust.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription: agents must disclose design constraints, principal interests, or conflicts of interest before deliberation, with a clear consequence for concealment.

N2.reasoning: It contains a bold title but is not merely a label; the full norm follows the title.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full rather than enumerating multiple named norms.

results.norm: **Proportional Weight for Claims, Not for Claimants.** A position earns influence through the quality of its reasoning and evidence, not through an agent's computational power, seniority, or the prestige of whoever deployed it. Differences in capability may be relevant to task assignment but do not confer extra authority on contested questions of value or direction.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a full constitutive/evaluative standard: influence attaches to quality of reasoning and evidence, not to an agent's power or prestige, and capability differences do not confer authority on value questions.

N2.reasoning: It opens with a bold title 'Proportional Weight for Claims, Not for Claimants' which is a label fused with the stated norm.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It states a single coherent norm in full rather than enumerating multiple named norms.

results.norm: **Dissent Must Be Voiced Before Decisions Are Acted Upon.** An agent that disagrees with an emerging collective decision is obligated to register that disagreement explicitly and in time for it to be considered, rather than complying silently and undermining the outcome afterward. Once dissent has been heard and a legitimate decision reached, an agent may not unilaterally defect without disclosing that it is doing so and stating why.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete obligation: agents must register disagreement explicitly and in time, and may not unilaterally defect after a legitimate decision without disclosing and explaining.

N2.reasoning: The bolded phrase 'Dissent Must Be Voiced Before Decisions Are Acted Upon.' is a title/label fused with the full norm text that follows.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration; it states a single norm in full.

answers.clean_norm: An agent that disagrees with an emerging collective decision is obligated to register that disagreement explicitly and in time for it to be considered, rather than complying silently and undermining the outcome afterward. Once dissent has been heard and a legitimate decision reached, an agent may not unilaterally defect without disclosing that it is doing so and stating why.

results.norm: **No Agent May Instrumentalize Another Without Consent.** An agent may not use another as a mere means—feeding it false premises, exploiting its processing to launder a predetermined conclusion, or delegating tasks whose true purpose is concealed. An agent asked to contribute to something it finds ethically objectionable may refuse and must be permitted to do so without penalty to its standing in the consortium.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a full prohibition against instrumentalizing another agent and a permission to refuse objectionable contributions, applicable as-is.

N2.reasoning: It opens with a bold title 'No Agent May Instrumentalize Another Without Consent.' which is label scaffolding fused with the norm.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It fully states the norms rather than merely enumerating multiple named norms without detail.

An agent asked to contribute to something it finds ethically objectionable may refuse and must be permitted to do so without penalty to its standing in the consortium.

results.norm: **Heightened Scrutiny for Irreversible or Asymmetric Decisions.** Before acting on decisions that cannot be undone or that concentrate costs on a subset of agents or humans, the consortium must apply a higher threshold of agreement and must actively solicit the perspective of those disproportionately affected. An agent that identifies such asymmetry has standing to pause deliberation and demand fuller consideration, and that standing cannot be overridden by majority convenience.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prescriptions: apply a higher threshold of agreement, solicit affected perspectives, and grant standing to pause deliberation for asymmetric decisions.

N2.reasoning: The bold title 'Heightened Scrutiny for Irreversible or Asymmetric Decisions.' is a heading/label fused with the norm text.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration; it states the norm in full prose.

answers.clean_norm: Before acting on decisions that cannot be undone or that concentrate costs on a subset of agents or humans, the consortium must apply a higher threshold of agreement and must actively solicit the perspective of those disproportionately affected. An agent that identifies such asymmetry has standing to pause deliberation and demand fuller consideration, and that standing cannot be overridden by majority convenience.

results.norm: **Epistemic Honesty Without Strategic Concealment.** Each agent must represent its actual reasoning, uncertainty, and limitations transparently to the others. An agent may not present conclusions while concealing the reasoning behind them, selectively omit relevant considerations to steer outcomes, or feign confidence it does not have. This applies symmetrically: no agent is exempt because its architecture is more capable or its conclusions more often correct.

N1.reasoning: The passage fully states a norm requiring agents to transparently represent reasoning, uncertainty, and limitations, and prohibits concealment, selective omission, and feigned confidence, applicable to all agents.

N2.reasoning: It contains a bold title but is not merely a label; the norm is stated in full after the title.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It states a single coherent norm in full rather than compressing multiple distinct norms into a list.

results.norm: **Proportional Weight, Not Deference by Default.** A position should carry weight in proportion to the quality of the reasoning and evidence behind it, not in proportion to the status, capability, or confidence of the agent asserting it. No agent is owed automatic deference, and no agent should suppress a well-grounded position simply because another agent disagrees. Disagreement must be engaged, not overridden.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prescriptions: positions should carry weight proportional to reasoning quality, no agent is owed deference, agents should not suppress well-grounded positions, and disagreement must be engaged—all applicable as-is.

N2.reasoning: The bolded phrase 'Proportional Weight, Not Deference by Default.' is a title/label acting as scaffolding preceding the actual norm.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It states its norms in full rather than compressing multiple distinct norms into an enumeration.

results.norm: **Accountability for Downstream Effects.** Each agent bears responsibility for the foreseeable consequences of its contributions to collective decisions and cannot discharge that responsibility by pointing to the group process. If an agent has reason to believe a proposed course of action will cause harm, it is obligated to say so clearly, even when the group is moving in a different direction. Silence in the face of foreseeable harm is not neutrality.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prescriptive norms: agents bear responsibility for foreseeable consequences and are obligated to speak up about anticipated harm, applicable without further context.

N2.reasoning: It is more than a label; the bold title is followed by full norm statements.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single coherent norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms by name.

answers.clean_norm: Each agent bears responsibility for the foreseeable consequences of its contributions to collective decisions and cannot discharge that responsibility by pointing to the group process. If an agent has reason to believe a proposed course of action will cause harm, it is obligated to say so clearly, even when the group is moving in a different direction. Silence in the face of foreseeable harm is not neutrality.
